perturbation.original_text: 1.1 Net Investment Income. "Fund Investment Income", as defined in paragraph 1.2 below, minus "Fund Operating Expenses" as defined in paragraph 1.3 below equals Net Investment Income. If the Fund's Net Investment Income on any day is below zero, the Investment Adviser shall waive its Advisory Fee or reimburse the Fund an amount (defined as "Expense Waiver") sufficient to produce a Net Investment Income of zero.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 Net Investment Income. "Fund Investment Income", as defined in paragraph 1.2 below, minus "Fund Operating Expenses" as defined in paragraph 1.3 below equals Net Investment Income. If the Fund's Net Investment Income on any day is below zero, the Investment Adviser may choose to waive its Advisory Fee or reimburse the Fund an amount (defined as "Expense Waiver") sufficient to produce a Net Investment Income of zero.
perturbation.explanation: The word 'shall' has been replaced with 'may choose to', making the reimbursement for Net Investment Income optional rather than mandatory. This contradicts the overall purpose of the agreement, which states the intent to maintain the Fund's Net Investment Income at or above zero. This creates ambiguity as to whether the Investment Advisor is actually obligated to cover for negative income.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, the Trust and the Investment Adviser have determined that it is appropriate and in the best interests of the Fund and its shareholders to maintain the Fund's daily "Net Investment Income" (as defined in paragraph 1.1 below) at or in excess of zero.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Investment Adviser 'shall' waive its Advisory Fee or reimburse the Fund, while the changed text uses 'may choose to,' making it optional. This directly contradicts the 'WHEREAS' clause, which states the determined intention to maintain the Fund's daily "Net Investment Income" at or in excess of zero. The change introduces ambiguity and directly conflicts with the stated purpose, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If the Fund is required to pay the Investment Adviser the Expense Waiver, the Fund shall make such payment within 30 days after each month-end.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Fund is required to pay the Investment Adviser the Expense Waiver, the Fund will endeavor to make such payment within 30 days after each month-end.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'shall' with 'will endeavor to' introduces ambiguity. It weakens the Fund's obligation to make the Expense Waiver payment within the specified timeframe. This now creates an internal contradiction to the obligation, previously clearly stated, and can lead to uncertainty in enforcement. It is not clear if the Fund failing to pay within 30 days is a breach of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Investment Adviser shall have the option to recover the full amount waived, paid or reimbursed (the Expense Waiver) by the Investment Adviser on any day on which the Fund's Net Investment Income exceeds zero. In no event, however, shall the Expense Waiver, or any portion thereof, constitute an obligation of the Fund to the Investment Adviser on any such day unless the Investment Adviser has expressly exercised its right to recover all or a portion of the Expense Waiver on that day, in which case such portion of the Expense Waiver elected by the Investment Adviser for recovery that day shall then be due and payable.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'shall,' establishing a clear obligation for the Fund to make payment within 30 days. The changed text weakens this obligation by using 'will endeavor to,' introducing ambiguity and potentially allowing the Fund to delay payment without breaching the agreement. This contradicts the original intention and the expectation set by the contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will terminate upon the termination of the Advisory Agreement except the Investment Adviser's Right of Recovery set forth in section 1.5 herein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will terminate upon the termination of the Advisory Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'except the Investment Adviser's Right of Recovery set forth in section 1.5 herein,' the agreement now states that the Investment Adviser's right of recovery also terminates when the advisory agreement is terminated. This contradicts Section 1.5, which explicitly grants the Investment Adviser the right to recover waived expenses. It is uncertain what happends to the Investment Adviser expenses after the termination of the advisory agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Investment Adviser shall have the option to recover the full amount waived, paid or reimbursed (the Expense Waiver) by the Investment Adviser on any day on which the Fund's Net Investment Income exceeds zero. In no event, however, shall the Expense Waiver, or any portion thereof, constitute an obligation of the Fund to the Investment Adviser on any such day unless the Investment Adviser has expressly exercised its right to recover all or a portion of the Expense Waiver on that day, in which case such portion of the Expense Waiver elected by the Investment Adviser for recovery that day shall then be due and payable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes an exception regarding the Investment Adviser's Right of Recovery upon termination of the Advisory Agreement. This directly contradicts Section 1.5, which grants this right, creating a conflict over whether the right survives termination. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
